_schema._comment: Reference schema for adding new providers. Not consumed by the OAuthHandler (the leading underscore is the ignore marker).
_schema._lookup_order: Slice 2.5 (locked 2026-06-15): per-project secure/projects/<active>/data/oauth-presets.json wins over this admin catalogue at PROVIDER level (full-entry replace, not field-level merge). Most projects use admin's catalogue as-is; per-project file only when a project needs custom providers, modified scope, or different extra_authorize_params.
_schema.userinfo_sub_path: <dot-path into the userinfo JSON for the user's unique id>
_schema.userinfo_email_path: <dot-path into the userinfo JSON for the user's email>
_schema.userinfo_name_path: <dot-path into the userinfo JSON for the user's display name; optional>
_schema.extra_authorize_params: <object of additional query params to append to the authorize URL; provider-specific (e.g. Google's access_type=offline)>
_schema.refresh_token_supported: <true if the provider issues refresh tokens with the standard flow; false for providers like GitHub OAuth Apps that don't expire tokens by default>
_schema.revoke_url: <OPTIONAL provider token-revocation endpoint per RFC 7009. When present, oauth-logout POSTs the access_token here with client_secret_basic auth. Omit for providers that don't implement RFC 7009 (e.g., GitHub uses a non-standard URL pattern; Meta uses a Graph API DELETE call) — logout falls back to local-only session clear in that case. Slice 2e (locked 2026-06-15).

test-oauth._comment: Local development fixture — quick-api-oauth (http://test.oauth) acts as the auth server, quick-api-route (http://test.api) as the resource server. Confidential client (test-client / test-secret) — exercises confidential + PKCE which matches the real-provider shape. Not for production use.
